Annual Compliance Reminder
ESOP Plan Document & Filing Status
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
ERISA Counsel
{{counselName}}
Compliance Year
{{complianceYear}}
Purpose: This annual reminder outlines the key compliance deadlines, plan document review items, and regulatory updates that require ERISA counsel attention for the {{complianceYear}} plan year. Please review and confirm receipt or flag any items requiring discussion.
§ 01Plan Document Review

The following plan document items should be reviewed annually to ensure the {{companyName}} ESOP remains in compliance with current law and IRS requirements:

A.Restatement cycle. Confirm whether the plan is on the current IRS pre-approved document restatement cycle. If a restatement is due, coordinate timing with the plan administrator.
B.Regulatory amendments. Review any new legislation or IRS guidance issued during {{complianceYear}} that requires a plan amendment (e.g., SECURE 2.0 provisions, cost-of-living adjustments, required minimum distribution age changes).
C.Prototype vs. custom document. Confirm the current document type and whether any conversion or update is advisable based on the company's evolving needs.
D.Summary Plan Description (SPD). Verify the SPD is current and reflects all adopted amendments. If amendments were adopted since the last SPD, a Summary of Material Modifications (SMM) is required.
§ 02Annual Filing Deadlines

The following filings and distributions are required for the {{complianceYear}} plan year:

Filing / Action
Deadline
Status
Form 5500 filing
{{form5500Deadline}}
{{form5500Status}}
SAR distribution to participants
{{sarDeadline}}
{{sarStatus}}
Annual participant statements
{{statementDeadline}}
{{statementStatus}}
Employer stock contribution
{{contributionDeadline}}
{{contributionStatus}}
Trustee certification of value
{{valuationDeadline}}
{{valuationStatus}}
§ 03Regulatory Updates

The following regulatory developments may affect the {{companyName}} ESOP and should be reviewed by counsel:

A.DOL guidance. {{dolGuidanceSummary}}
B.IRS updates. {{irsUpdatesSummary}}
C.Legislative developments. {{legislativeSummary}}
Action needed: Please confirm whether any of the above require a plan amendment, operational change, or filing adjustment for {{companyName}}.
§ 04Plan Amendments

If any amendments are required based on the regulatory review above or operational changes during the year, they should be documented and adopted before the applicable deadline:

A.Discretionary amendments. Any amendments adopted at the direction of the plan sponsor (e.g., changes to eligibility, vesting, matching formula) must be documented with board resolution.
B.Required amendments. Amendments mandated by law or IRS guidance must be adopted by the regulatory deadline, typically the end of the plan year following the year the change is effective.
C.Retroactive amendments. If any amendments are needed retroactively, coordinate with counsel on correction procedures under EPCRS or VCP as applicable.
§ 05Upcoming Events

The following events are anticipated during the upcoming plan year that may require counsel involvement:

A.Diversification elections. Participants who have completed 10 years of service or reached age 55 are eligible for diversification rights. Confirm distribution of election notices by {{diversificationNoticeDeadline}}.
B.Distribution events. {{distributionEventsSummary}}
C.Repurchase obligation triggers. {{repurchaseTriggersSummary}}
§ 06Action Items

The following items require ERISA counsel attention. Requested completion dates are noted:

Next step: Please confirm receipt of this reminder and advise of any items that require a call or additional coordination.
